The period that is excluded under subsection (13.7) is to be included in the calculation of the 104 weeks for the purposes of subsection (14).
Subsections 10(13.1) to (14.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:
Subject to subsection (15), an extension under any of subsections (10) to (13) must not result in a benefit period of more than 104 weeks.
Subsection 12(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsections (2.1) to (2.6), the maximum number of weeks for which benefits may be paid in a benefit period because of a reason other than those mentioned in subsection (3) shall be determined in accordance with the table in Schedule I by reference to the regional rate of unemployment that applies to the claimant and the number of hours of insurable employment of the claimant in their qualifying period.

Subject to subsection (2.7), the number of weeks of benefits set out in the table in Schedule I that applies in respect of a claimant is deemed to be the number of weeks that would otherwise apply in respect of the claimant, but for this subsection, increased by five weeks if the following conditions are met:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant is not a long-tenured worker;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant’s benefit period began during the period beginning on January 4, 2015 and ending on July 8, 2017;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant’s ordinary residence at the beginning of the benefit period was in a region referred to in subsection (2.8); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: benefits were paid or payable to the claimant because of a reason mentioned in subsection (2) for at least one week in the benefit period.

Subsection.Text: If subsection (2.1) applies in respect of a claimant whose benefit period is deemed under subsection 10(13.2) not to have ended,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant may, for weeks beginning on or after July 3, 2016, be paid benefits because of a reason mentioned in subsection (2) for no more than the five additional weeks referred to in subsection (2.1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant may not be paid those additional five weeks of benefits for any week that began before July 3, 2016.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2.7), the number of weeks of benefits set out in the table in Schedule I that applies in respect of a claimant is deemed to be the number of weeks that would otherwise apply in respect of the claimant, but for this subsection, increased by 25 weeks if the following conditions are met:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant is a long-tenured worker;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant’s benefit period began during the period beginning on January 4, 2015 and ending on October 29, 2016;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant’s ordinary residence at the beginning of the benefit period was in a region referred to in subsection (2.8); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: benefits were paid or payable to the claimant because of a reason mentioned in subsection (2) for at least one week in the benefit period.

Subsection.Text: If subsection (2.3) applies in respect of a claimant whose benefit period is deemed under subsection 10(13.4) not to have ended,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant may, for weeks beginning on or after July 3, 2016, be paid benefits because of a reason mentioned in subsection (2) for no more than the 25 additional weeks referred to in subsection (2.3); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant may not be paid those additional 25 weeks of benefits for any week that began before July 3, 2016.

Subsection.Text: The number of weeks of benefits set out in the table in Schedule I that applies in respect of a claimant is deemed to be the number of weeks that would otherwise apply in respect of the claimant, but for this subsection, increased by 17 weeks if the following conditions are met:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant is a long-tenured worker;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant’s benefit period began during the period beginning on October 30, 2016 and ending on February 25, 2017;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant’s ordinary residence at the beginning of the benefit period was in a region referred to in subsection (2.8); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: benefits were paid or payable to the claimant because of a reason mentioned in subsection (2) for at least one week in the benefit period.

Subsection.Text: The number of weeks of benefits set out in the table in Schedule I that applies in respect of a claimant is deemed to be the number of weeks that would otherwise apply in respect of the claimant, but for this subsection, increased by 10 weeks if the following conditions are met:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant is a long-tenured worker;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant’s benefit period began during the period beginning on February 26, 2017 and ending on July 8, 2017;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the claimant’s ordinary residence at the beginning of the benefit period was in a region referred to in subsection (2.8); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: benefits were paid or payable to the claimant because of a reason mentioned in subsection (2) for at least one week in the benefit period.

Subsection.Text: If more than one benefit period in respect of a claimant began before July 3, 2016, subsection (2.1) or (2.3), as the case may be, applies to increase the number of weeks of benefits only in the benefit period that began on the day that is closest to that day.
The regions, for the purposes of subsections (2.1) to (2.6), are the following regions described in Schedule I to the Employment Insurance Regulations:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the region of Northern Ontario described in subsection 2(3) of that Schedule;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the region of Sudbury described in subsection 2(14) of that Schedule;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the region of Northern Manitoba described in subsection 6(3) of that Schedule;

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: the region of Southern Interior British Columbia described in subsection 7(1) of that Schedule;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the region of Northern British Columbia described in subsection 7(5) of that Schedule;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the region of Saskatoon described in subsection 9(2) of that Schedule;

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: the region of Southern Saskatchewan described in subsection 9(3) of that Schedule;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the region of Northern Saskatchewan described in subsection 9(4) of that Schedule;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the region of Calgary described in subsection 10(1) of that Schedule;

Paragraph.Label: (g.1)
Paragraph.Text: the region of Edmonton described in subsection 10(2) of that Schedule;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the region of Southern Alberta described in subsection 10(3) of that Schedule;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the region of Northern Alberta described in subsection 10(4) of that Schedule;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the region of Newfoundland/Labrador described in subsection 11(2) of that Schedule;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: the region of Whitehorse described in subsection 12(1) of that Schedule; and

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: the region of Nunavut described in subsection 14(2) of that Schedule.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 12(2) to (2.8) of the Act are replaced by the following:

The maximum number of weeks for which benefits may be paid in a benefit period because of a reason other than those mentioned in subsection (3) shall be determined in accordance with the table in Schedule I by reference to the regional rate of unemployment that applies to the claimant and the number of hours of insurable employment of the claimant in their qualifying period.
Subsection 12(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

In a claimant’s benefit period, the claimant may, subject to the applicable maximums, combine weeks of benefits to which the claimant is entitled because of a reason mentioned in subsections (2) and (3), but the total number of weeks of benefits shall not exceed 50 or, if the maximum number of weeks for which benefits may be paid to a claimant because of a reason mentioned in subsection (2) is greater than 45 weeks as a result of the application of any of subsections (2.1), (2.3), (2.5) and (2.6), the number that corresponds to that maximum number of weeks increased by five weeks.
Subsection 12(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

In a claimant’s benefit period, the claimant may, subject to the applicable maximums, combine weeks of benefits to which the claimant is entitled because of a reason mentioned in subsections (2) and (3), but the total number of weeks of benefits shall not exceed 50.

Section.Text: Section 13 of the Act is replaced by the following:

A claimant is not entitled to be paid benefits in a benefit period until, after the beginning of the benefit period, the claimant has served a waiting period of one week of unemployment for which benefits would otherwise be payable.
Subsection 22(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of section 13, the provisions of section 18 do not apply to the week that immediately precedes the period described in subsection (2).

Section.Text: Section 54 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (z.3) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (z.31)
Paragraph.Text: eliminating the special benefits payable to a claimant in respect of any period that constitutes, under a plan other than one established under a provincial law, an elimination period during which no benefit is payable to the claimant under the plan; and

Section.Text: Section 58 of the Act is replaced by the following:

In this Part, insured participant means an insured person who requests assistance under employment benefits and, when requesting the assistance, is an unemployed person for whom a benefit period is established or whose benefit period has ended within the previous 60 months.
Subsection 63(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An agreement may be entered into under subsection (1) with a government even if the benefits provided by that government are provided only for an insured participant as defined in section 58 as it read immediately before June 23, 2015, the text of which is set out in Schedule III.

Section.Text: Section 63.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If an agreement that is entered into with a government under section 63 provides for the payment of contributions for all or a portion of the costs of benefits provided by the government that are similar to employment benefits under this Part and the benefits to be provided by that government under the agreement are provided only for an insured participant as defined in section 58 as it read immediately before June 23, 2015, the text of which is set out in Schedule III, the contributions to be paid under the agreement shall be paid only for costs of benefits for an insured participant as defined in that section 58.
Subsection (1) applies in respect of agreements entered into before, on or after the day on which this subsection comes into force.

Section.Text: The definition waiting period in subsection 152.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: waiting period means the one week of the benefit period described in section 152.15. (délai de carence)

Section.Text: Subsection 152.07(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A violation may not be taken into account under paragraph (1)(d) in more than two initial claims by an individual for benefits under this Act if the individual who accumulated the violation qualified for benefits in each of those two initial claims, taking into account subparagraph (1)(d)(ii), subsection 7.1(1) or regulations made under Part VIII, as the case may be.

Section.Text: Section 152.15 of the Act is replaced by the following:

A self-employed person is not entitled to be paid benefits in a benefit period until, after the beginning of the benefit period, the person has served a waiting period of one week of unemployment for which benefits would otherwise be payable.
Schedule I to the Act is amended by replacing the reference after the heading “SCHEDULE I” with the following:
Schedule I to the Act is amended by replacing the references after the heading “SCHEDULE I” with the following:
The Act is amended by adding, after Schedule II, the Schedule III set out in Schedule 2 to this Act.

The following provisions of the Employment Insurance Act, as those provisions read immediately before July 9, 2017, continue to apply in respect of a claimant, as defined in subsection 2(1) of that Act, whose benefit period, as defined in that subsection 2(1), began before that day and has not ended before that day:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the definition long-tenured worker in subsection 2(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 10(13.1) to (14.1); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 12(2) to (2.8) and (6).

Section.Text: The following provisions of the Employment Insurance Act, as enacted by subsections 207(3), 209(1) and 210(1) and (3) and section 220, apply only in respect of a claimant, as defined in subsection 2(1) of that Act, whose benefit period, as defined in that subsection 2(1), begins on or after the day fixed by order of the Governor in Council made under subsection 231(3):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 2(5);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 7(2) before paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 7.1(1) before the table and subsection 7.1(3); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 152.07(7).

Section.Text: The following provisions of the Employment Insurance Act, as those provisions read immediately before the day fixed by order of the Governor in Council made under subsection 231(4), continue to apply in respect of a claimant, as defined in subsection 2(1) of that Act, whose benefit period, as defined in that subsection 2(1), began before that day:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the definition waiting period in subsection 6(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 13;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 22(4);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the definition waiting period in subsection 152.01(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: section 152.15.

Section.Text: Regulations made by the Canada Employment Insurance Commission under the Employment Insurance Act that, in the opinion of the Commission, are necessary as a result of the amendments made by sections 208, 213, 214, 219 and 221 may, if the regulations so provide, be retroactive and have effect with respect to any period before they are made that begins on or after the day that is fixed by order of the Governor in Council made under subsection 231(4).

Subsections 153(3) and (4) of the Employment Insurance Act do not apply in respect of any regulations made by the Canada Employment Insurance Commission under that Act that, in the opinion of the Commission, are necessary as a result of the amendments made by subsection 207(3) and sections 209, 210, 216, 220 and 230.

Heading.TitleText: Consequential Amendment to the Economic Action Plan 2015 Act, No. 1

Section.Text: Section 158 of the Economic Action Plan 2015 Act, No. 1 is repealed.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 207(1), 211(1) and 212(1) and (3) and section 222 come into force or are deemed to have come into force on July 3, 2016.
Subsections 207(2), 211(2) and 212(2) and (4) and section 223 come into force on July 9, 2017.
Subsection 207(3) and sections 209, 210, 216, 220 and 230 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Sections 208, 213, 214, 219 and 221 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, which may be earlier than the day on which the order is made but not earlier than January 1, 2017.

Section.Text: The Canada Marine Act is amended by adding the following after section 25.1:

Despite section 25, the Minister of Canadian Heritage may make payments to Canada Place Corporation for Canada Day celebrations and for the celebrations marking the 150th anniversary of Confederation.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Jobs, Growth and Long-term Prosperity Act

Section.Text: The Jobs, Growth and Long-term Prosperity Act is amended by adding the following after section 209:

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph 90(1)(b) of the Financial Administration Act, the Minister of Infrastructure, Communities and Intergovernmental Affairs may acquire the shares of PPP Canada Inc.
The appropriate Minister holds the shares acquired under subsection (1).

The appropriate Minister may, with the approval of the Governor in Council, conduct any transaction referred to in any of paragraphs 90(1)(c) to (e) of the Financial Administration Act in respect of PPP Canada Inc.
PPP Canada Inc. may, with the approval of the Governor in Council, sell or otherwise dispose of all or substantially all of its assets.

Any of the corporations that are part of the group of corporations that consists of PPP Canada Inc. and of all of its wholly-owned subsidiaries may, with the approval of the Governor in Council, sell or otherwise dispose of any of the assets of the corporations, even if the assets to be sold or otherwise disposed of constitute all or substantially all of the total assets of that group.

Section.Text: Section 211 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (b), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: conducting any activity specified in an order made under section 211.1.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 211:
The Governor in Council may, by order, specify any activity in relation to which PPP Canada Inc. is an agent of Her Majesty in right of Canada.
Section 213 of the Act is repealed.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 15
Heading.TitleText: Canada Foundation for Sustainable Development Technology

TitleText.XRefExternal: Canada Foundation for Sustainable Development Technology Act

Section.Text: Paragraphs 9(2)(a) and (b) of the Canada Foundation for Sustainable Development Technology Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Chairperson of the board appointed by the Governor in Council on the Minister’s recommendation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: six persons appointed by the Governor in Council on the Minister’s recommendation; and

Section.Text: Section 143 of the Budget Implementation Act, 2007 is replaced by the following:

There may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of Industry, a sum not exceeding $200 million to the Canada Foundation for Sustainable Development Technology for its use.

Despite section 143, the sum that may be paid out under that section is not to exceed the difference between $200 million and the sum paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister of the Environment and the Minister of Natural Resources to the Canada Foundation for Sustainable Development Technology for its use, under section 143 as it read immediately before the day on which this section comes into force.

ScheduleFormHeading.Label: SCHEDULE III
ScheduleFormHeading.TitleText: Text of Section 58 as It Read Before June 23, 2015

Subsection.Text: In this Part, insured participant means an insured person who requests assistance under employment benefits and, when requesting the assistance, is an unemployed person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for whom a benefit period is established or whose benefit period has ended within the previous 36 months; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for whom a benefit period has been established in the previous 60 months and who

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: was paid special benefits under section 22 or 23 during the benefit period,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsequently withdrew from active participation in the labour force to care for one or more of their new-born children or one or more children placed with them for the purpose of adoption, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: is seeking to re-enter the labour force.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), benefit period includes a benefit period established under the Unemployment Insurance Act and special benefits includes benefits under sections 18 and 20 of that Act.
